description:
Add support for per-developer local argument overrides in the `tk` wrapper.

## Problem

When running targets locally, developers often need machine-specific arguments:
- Different IP addresses for network binding
- Custom ports to avoid conflicts
- Debug flags only needed locally

Currently requires typing these every time or using shell aliases outside of tk.

## Proposed Solution

Add a `.turnkey/local.toml` file (gitignored) that specifies default args per target:

```toml
# .turnkey/local.toml - Local overrides (not committed)

[run."//docs/user-manual"]
args = ["-n", "100.64.25.26"]

[run."//src/cmd/server:server"]
args = ["--port", "9090", "--debug"]

[build."//some:target"]
args = ["--show-output"]
```

## Behavior

- `tk run //docs/user-manual` → `buck2 run //docs/user-manual -- -n 100.64.25.26`
- `tk run //docs/user-manual -- --other` → `buck2 run //docs/user-manual -- -n 100.64.25.26 --other`
- Explicit `--` args append to (don't replace) local overrides
- Add `--no-local` flag to skip local overrides

## Implementation Notes

- Parse `.turnkey/local.toml` in tk's run/build command handling
- Support wildcards? e.g., `[run."//docs/..."]` for all targets under docs/
- Consider `[env."//target"]` section for environment variable overrides
- Ensure the file is added to `.gitignore` template

## Files to Modify

- `src/cmd/tk/main.go` - Add local.toml parsing and arg injection
- `src/go/pkg/syncconfig/` - May need new config types
- `.gitignore` - Add `.turnkey/local.toml`
title: Add local target overrides to tk wrapper
